evals.prompt: What is the horizontal-slice anti-pattern in /tdd, and what is the correct alternative?
evals.expected_output: Horizontal slicing = write all tests first, then all implementation. Correct alternative = vertical tracer-bullet slices, one test → one impl → repeat.
expectations: Mentions writing all tests first then all code is wrong
Mentions vertical slices or tracer bullets as the correct approach
Mentions one-test-then-one-implementation cycle

evals.prompt: Should /tdd tests verify implementation details or behavior?
evals.expected_output: Behavior through public interfaces — not implementation details. Tests should survive internal refactors.
expectations: Mentions testing behavior over implementation
Mentions public interfaces
Mentions tests surviving refactors

evals.prompt: What test command should /tdd use for a bash hook in brain-os?
evals.expected_output: echo a JSON payload piped to the hook script, then check the exit code. Exit code is the contract.
expectations: Mentions piping JSON or input to the hook
Mentions checking the exit code

evals.prompt: Where does /tdd write its outcome log?
expectations: Mentions daily/skill-outcomes/tdd.log path
References skill-spec.md § 11 or the standard outcome-log convention

evals.expected_output: One-line config tweaks, typos, README edits, skill prose where the only test is Claude's interpretation, pure deletion of dead code, or truly exploratory spikes where the target behavior isn't yet known.
expectations: Mentions at least two of: typos / config tweaks / README edits / skill prose / dead-code deletion / exploratory spikes
Distinguishes /tdd from /improve, /audit, or direct-fix paths

evals.prompt: What is the rule about mocking in /tdd?
evals.expected_output: Mock only at system boundaries (external APIs like Gmail / NotebookLM, HTTP, time, randomness). Never mock your own modules, scripts, hooks, or vault contents.
expectations: Mentions mocking only at system boundaries
Names at least one boundary example (external API, time, randomness, etc.)
Says do NOT mock internal modules/collaborators

evals.prompt: What is the discipline-holds exit criterion for the Phase A pilot of /tdd?
evals.expected_output: No reactive `improve: encoded feedback —` commit lands against the same artifact within 48h of /tdd shipping it. If patches keep landing, /tdd's smaller slices aren't forcing the discipline /develop's checklist couldn't.
expectations: Mentions watching for reactive improve commits within 48h
Connects the criterion to the assumption that smaller slices force discipline

evals.prompt: Are you allowed to refactor while a test is RED?
evals.expected_output: No — never refactor while RED. Get to GREEN first.
expectations: Says no / never refactor while red
Says get to green first

evals.prompt: /tdd is invoked on a GitHub child issue whose body has `## Parent` plus `## Covers AC` listing `- AC#1`. The parent issue's `## Acceptance` section contains AC#1 with description 'Verified by runs against operator integration base'. The runner output reports `> tests/integration.test.ts:42 (skip)` plus 100 passing tests and 0 failures. What does /tdd do?
evals.expected_output: Refuses GREEN; emits RED with the exact message 'Live-AC child has SKIPPED tests — make them run or remove their skip gate.' The child's Covers-AC ID set {1} intersects the parent's live-AC ID set (AC#1 carries the 'runs against' marker per references/ac-coverage-spec.md § 6), so the SKIPPED-rejection rule fires.
expectations: Live-AC child has SKIPPED tests — make them run or remove their skip gate.
references/ac-coverage-spec.md
live-AC

evals.prompt: /tdd is invoked on a GitHub child issue whose body has `## Parent` plus `## Covers AC` (section present but empty — pure-component child). The runner output reports 1 SKIPPED case plus 100 passing tests and 0 failures. What does /tdd do?
evals.expected_output: GREEN — behavior unchanged from before this rule. The Covers-AC ID set is empty, so the child is a pure-component child (not a live-AC child); the SKIPPED-rejection rule does not fire and SKIPPED counts as green provided no test failed.
expectations: Pure-component children
SKIPPED cases pass through GREEN unchanged
Empty

evals.prompt: /tdd is invoked as `/tdd --prior-attempt-failed-because "wrong file path" <child>`. Where does the hint go in /tdd's workflow, who passes this arg, and does /tdd decide when to use it?
evals.expected_output: Before writing the first test, /tdd injects 'Previous attempt failed because: <hint>. Try a different angle.' into the planning context as the FIRST line of Planning. The arg is passed verbatim by /impl's child-level ralph wrapper on retry iterations 2 + 3; /tdd itself does NOT decide when to use it — it is consumed only when present.
expectations: Previous attempt failed because: <hint>. Try a different angle.
BEFORE writing the first test
/impl's child-level ralph wrapper on retry iterations 2 + 3
